126626.explanation: The Wassenaar VVD has the highest number of women candidates in March! We consider this important. We are against mandatory quotas based on any criteria; (administrative) quality always comes first.

126627.explanation: We speak daily with Wassenaar residents about what is going well and what can be improved. We want genuine participation: visiting neighborhood associations, input on projects in your own area, or with entrepreneurs. This happens through dialogue-not through referenda with only yes/no questions.

126629.explanation: Entrepreneurs must have the freedom to do business. That is our starting point. We appreciate our farmers, and activities supporting their farming operations are negotiable under conditions.

126630.explanation: We support encouraging, informing, and facilitating Wassenaar residents in sustainability and the energy transition. Voluntary and affordable! We wholeheartedly support many great neighborhood initiatives, such as around Paauw, Schouwweg, and others.

126633.explanation: Wassenaar must remain green! That is one of the unique qualities that make Wassenaar a pleasant village to live, work, and grow up in. No debate.

126636.explanation: If the nationwide fireworks ban takes effect in 2027, we will organize a fireworks show so Wassenaar residents can continue to enjoy this tradition. Safe and responsible for residents and emergency workers! The bonfire in Kerkehout must remain!

126637.explanation: We want customer-friendly, high-quality service at the lowest possible cost for Wassenaar residents. Costs for waste should not rise. Bulky waste must be collected free of charge for residents.

126639.explanation: Our disabled residents must be able to access everywhere. It's a matter of decency.

126640.explanation: This depends on the project, neighborhood, number, and type of houses. We do not say 'no' in advance to all cases. Too many Wassenaar residents are stuck or without housing to prohibit this entirely.

126641.explanation: Sports directly contribute to health, self-confidence, and social participation. By reimbursing memberships, people on social assistance can also stay active and build structure.

126644.explanation: We do not want to make promises we cannot keep. Music lessons are valuable. At the same time, not every activity can be subsidized by the municipality. Every euro of tax money can only be spent once.

126648.explanation: Everyone must be able to walk safely on the streets at any time of day. Camera surveillance in high-risk areas can help prevent crime and enable faster response to incidents. This increases safety in Wassenaar and the feeling of safety among residents.

126649.explanation: Victims of domestic violence must always receive quick and safe help. The most important thing is that help is accessible and professionally arranged, whether in Wassenaar itself or through regional shelters. The key is sufficient accommodation for those who need it.

126650.explanation: Too many starters have nowhere to go and cannot move without support. That is why we want to build especially for starters and families in the middle segment. If that is truly insufficient, a temporary loan as a helping hand may be considered.

126653.explanation: It is your own home. This makes better use of existing houses. That helps ease housing pressure and gives Wassenaar residents more opportunities to find suitable housing.

126654.explanation: Building in places like the dunes or along the estate route is not an option for us. We want to build on existing locations and sites suitable for development to also meet the great demand for houses and apartments.

126655.explanation: We expect an effort from everyone living on social assistance. It is logical to see how you can give something back and contribute. It is a first step toward work and gives back to the community. No one is left on the sidelines.

126657.explanation: Work must always pay off! By increasing income policy at the local level, it becomes even less rewarding to move from benefits to a job. That can and should never be the intention. Vulnerable people are helped in other ways.

126658.explanation: Our youth work helps young people grow up safely and prevents problems. By continuing to invest in this, we prevent bigger issues later and ensure our youth in sometimes difficult home situations do not fall through the cracks.

126659.explanation: Not automatically. Charging spots are important and of course possible, but should not unnecessarily come at the expense of available parking spaces. We simply won't harass car owners by taking away their parking spots, and we do not support that.

126661.explanation: No one can fulfill this promise with the large shortage of personnel. Loving and professional care must be accessible to everyone and based on quality and professionalism. That is our priority.

126664.explanation: Dog owners pay for the care and responsibility of their loyal four-legged friend. Abolishing the dog tax is fairer if all dog owners actually clean up their dog poop.

126666.explanation: Yes! Many entrepreneurs in Wassenaar who want to stay here lack space and want to grow. No business space has been added in the last 30 years. The Maaldrift business park (our only site) can be smartly expanded without disadvantaging residents.

126668.explanation: We find it unfair that residents of Wassenaar wait for years on the housing list while refugees are prioritized. Both groups urgently need a home, so this priority rule does not fit.

126670.explanation: A small-scale amount of solar panels on agricultural land can contribute to the energy transition. A ban is too extreme. We believe it should remain possible, provided it is carefully integrated and does not unnecessarily damage the landscape.

126673.explanation: We want Wassenaar to remain a safe and healthy village for families, youth, and children. A coffee shop does not fit in with that.

126674.explanation: Our greatest priority lies in building for the middle groups; working starters, police officers, healthcare workers, starting entrepreneurs, or older people stuck in a bind. Therefore, we do not want only social rental housing but want to consider what is best per project and neighborhood.

126676.explanation: It is a false promise to build at least 10 extra neighborhood centers. Neighborhood associations are important, but not every neighborhood needs its own building or center. We prefer to spend our tax money to implement neighborhood initiatives more quickly.

126681.explanation: Against! Paid parking makes visiting the center less attractive. We want to keep the center accessible and lively for shopkeepers, hospitality, and all residents of Wassenaar. Some residents also really need their car.

126682.explanation: In new agreements with the COA regarding the Duinrell reception center, we want to reduce its size to better fit Wassenaar. We want the shelter to focus more on families. We take our responsibility but also take signals of insecurity seriously.

126626.explanation: HartvoorWassenaar wants good governance and sound financial policy so that Wassenaar's independence is guaranteed. Wassenaar must return to the top 10. HartvoorWassenaar wants very good aldermen and will nominate both strong female and male candidates.

126627.explanation: HartvoorWassenaar strongly supports the possibility of a referendum. Not for every trivial matter but for important decisions. For example, 30 km/h. A municipal council must always realize that the residents of Wassenaar are ultimately in charge. HartvoorWassenaar respects the residents of Wassenaar.

126629.explanation: What we find much more important is reducing cut-through traffic. For 40 years there has been a lot of nuisance from cut-through traffic without anything being done. A motion from HartvoorWassenaar was adopted. A first traffic dosing installation must be placed on Katwijkseweg. This will happen in 2026.

126630.explanation: It is important that the municipality ensures the energy transition takes place carefully in the coming years. No expensive experiments. Energy at the lowest possible cost. And make sure energy is always available. That is the best solution.

126633.explanation: Strongly agree. HartvoorWassenaar believes that no healthy tree should be cut down. Sick or dead trees must be replaced by new trees. HartvoorWassenaar wants to plant 1,000 extra trees in the next four years. Trees in every street! Wassenaar must be a very green municipality.

126636.explanation: Nice idea. From 2026 there will be a nationwide ban on setting off fireworks. Difficult to enforce, but an experiment with a fireworks show could be good. HartvoorWassenaar also wants enforcement to be strongly improved.

126637.explanation: Bulky waste can be brought to the recycling center. It is delivered there free of charge. Home collection of bulky waste is expensive. It is possible but not free. Individual responsibility. Avalex rates must not be increased further. They are already the highest in the Netherlands.

126639.explanation: It is the responsibility of entrepreneurs to ensure their building is accessible to everyone. They must do this for their customers, employees, and other visitors. It is part of entrepreneurship to take care of that.

126640.explanation: A farm is a farm. Houses do not belong there. The municipality is responsible for zoning in Wassenaar. Preserving green spaces and green zones is top priority. HartvoorWassenaar is careful with green and the green environment. Not everything can be allowed.

126641.explanation: It is a positive thought. But there are many wishes to support groups of people with money. The municipality already does quite a bit to help people. The municipality must be open when support is truly effective.

126644.explanation: There are many items on the wish list for which the municipality should allocate funds. The municipality already does a lot. But it will be difficult to finance all of it.

126648.explanation: Camera surveillance makes it possible to see what happens at certain points. This should help the police improve safety in Wassenaar. It must work as a deterrent towards potential offenders.

126649.explanation: We have not heard any signals that this is necessary. The municipality is capable of tackling and solving problem cases.

126650.explanation: The municipality has no role in financing homes. That is the job of mortgage lenders. It would lead to inequality and drive up housing prices. In practice, it is also difficult to implement. Do not do it.

126653.explanation: Wassenaar accepts the obligation to build its share of needed homes in the Netherlands. That is 75 per year, not the 1,000 the province has imposed on us. That only comes at the expense of green spaces. HartvoorWassenaar wants gradual growth of residents and homes - what we can handle.

126654.explanation: HartvoorWassenaar firmly supports a green municipality. No further building outside the existing village developments. Green must remain green. We have some beautiful green canals around Wassenaar. HartvoorWassenaar wants to do everything for a good quality of life.

126655.explanation: It would be good. Informal care, household help, shopping for elderly. Delivering mail, where the municipality receives compensation from PostNL. It will always be difficult to organize but worth the effort. Also for the meaningful engagement of people on social assistance.

126657.explanation: No direct reason to raise or lower that income limit. As it is now, it is good. Of course everyone would like more money, but the municipality must also have funds. Unless it would be at the expense of other good things.

126658.explanation: The costs of youth care have gotten completely out of hand. More than tripled compared to a few years ago. The goal must be to have the same quality youth care but spend less money. That is possible, as other municipalities have proven. It will require significant effort.

126659.explanation: Yes. There are more and more electric cars in the village and not everyone has a driveway. That means more charging stations are needed, well distributed throughout the village. There is a big parking problem in and around the center. That really must be tackled now. High priority.

126661.explanation: When possible, it should be tried. But there is a shortage in care, so it cannot always be organized. The available capacity must then be allocated without options.

126664.explanation: Dogs bring costs and nuisance. You can charge a price for that. Abolishing the tax means losing income, which must be compensated via property tax. Many people enjoy their dog a lot and it should not be made expensive. However, dogs are strong polluters as well.

126666.explanation: More business parks come at the expense of green spaces. There is no economic necessity to sacrifice green. Wassenaar is a green municipality that must be well protected.

126668.explanation: No. It cannot be that residents waiting for social rental housing must give priority to status holders. Wassenaar residents have first rights. Then come status holders.

126670.explanation: Green is valuable in a green municipality like Wassenaar. The sea view has already been spoiled by windmills. Do not spoil the green view with solar panels. It is important that the energy transition is well organized. No experiments. Guarantee energy at the lowest possible cost.

126673.explanation: Yes. It does not belong in Wassenaar. Wassenaar must return to the top 10 best municipalities to live in, having dropped from the top 10 to 32. A coffee shop does not belong here.

126674.explanation: For years, the norm of 30 percent social housing in new construction projects has applied in Wassenaar. We must maintain that. But with the condition that residents of Wassenaar have priority for a social rental home when one becomes available.

126676.explanation: There are currently three neighborhood centers: Kerkehout, Den Deijl, O&O. HartvoorWassenaar. There are a few neighborhoods where a neighborhood center could be. But they cost a lot of money, which makes it difficult.

126681.explanation: We are a village. Paid parking does not belong here. It solves little. We should not yet see it as (limited) income source for now.

126682.explanation: The dispersion law states that Wassenaar must accommodate 158 asylum seekers. Currently, there are 930. This decision was made without the council's involvement. The feeling of insecurity has increased enormously. HartvoorWassenaar wants to return to the old situation (only during the winter months) or a maximum of 158.

126626.explanation: A council consisting of five old men who tick all the boxes is not what we envision. Quality comes first, and diversity is part of that.

126627.explanation: If the subject lends itself to it, we ask the residents for their opinion. This can take the form of a referendum, but also a citizen's assembly, survey, or conversations with the residents.

126629.explanation: We welcome private initiatives. In principle, we support farmers seeking alternative sources of income and doing so as sustainably as possible. The answer 'always allow' goes too far, as there may be circumstances where it is not possible.

126630.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar welcomes it when residents want to generate sustainable energy together. The municipality has no subsidy options for this; the national government or the province do.

126633.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar wants Wassenaar to remain a green municipality. This increases livability and makes the soil resilient against heavy rainfall. In addition, this keeps Wassenaar cool during hot summers.

126636.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar believes that the municipality can organize something for New Year's Eve. This could be a light show, but possibly also a fireworks show. Hopefully, the ice rink will return as well.

126637.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar thinks that bulky waste can be collected free of charge at home once per quarter. This saves costs, means people no longer need to go to Avalex with vans, and prevents bulky waste from being illegally placed by the roadside.

126639.explanation: Where buildings are not easily accessible to people with disabilities, the municipality and entrepreneurs can cooperate. The municipality takes care of the section up to the door, and the entrepreneur pays for the part behind the door.

126640.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar’s principle is that the Green Ring around Wassenaar should remain. At the same time, we see that the agricultural sector is becoming less active, a certain deterioration threatens, and there is a large demand for housing. Small-scale housing construction can be a solution.

126641.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar believes everyone in Wassenaar should be able to participate in social activities. Membership of a sports club and library can help maintain social contacts. In this network, you can also get other forms of help or tips about a new job.

126644.explanation: Just like sports, cultural activities are very important for connection within our municipality. Here we meet each other, learn to cooperate, and make contacts, also for the future.

126648.explanation: Often, camera surveillance is not a solution for an unsafe situation. We believe much more in designing the village safely, with some form of social control, good public lighting, and supervision by police and enforcement officers.

126649.explanation: Domestic violence also occurs in Wassenaar.

126650.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar wants to give first-time homebuyers a chance by increasing the supply of homes. This is more effective than providing a starter loan, which would only drive up prices without more first-time buyers being able to buy a home.

126653.explanation: There is a housing shortage among Wassenaar residents as well. Using holiday homes could be a solution.

126654.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar wants to maintain the green ring around the village. In specific cases (for example, the ANWB location), homes can be built. There will be no large-scale developments (so no Valkenburg-style housing developments in Wassenaar).

126655.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar assumes that no one consciously chooses to end up on welfare (with exceptions). We believe people receiving welfare benefits should get help to support themselves again.

126657.explanation: We find the current income limit acceptable; it does not need to be adjusted.

126658.explanation: Youth work has proven its value. This keeps young people out of trouble, allows them to achieve a starting qualification, and enables them to live independently more often. It is a form of prevention that works well.

126659.explanation: More and more electric cars are being sold, more than petrol cars. It is therefore logical that the municipality designates more parking spaces for charging.

126661.explanation: Care in Wassenaar is religiously and culturally neutral. Don’t complain if the nurse has a different faith than you; care is care.

126664.explanation: The revenue from the dog tax is limited; it hardly outweighs the administrative costs.

126666.explanation: Space in Wassenaar is limited. Housing and green areas take priority over business parks. Companies already in Wassenaar can stay. There is no room for new large-scale projects in the next four years.

126668.explanation: This issue must be viewed broadly. It also matters that Wassenaar accommodates significantly more refugees than it must (four times as many). Perhaps Wassenaar can receive an exemption from the task of housing refugees in social rental homes.

126670.explanation: You cannot say this so generally. However, we do want the green character to remain really green. We therefore consider it undesirable that solar panels should be installed on all agricultural land.

126673.explanation: We believe coffeeshops do not fit a village like Wassenaar. They create a feeling of insecurity; we think Wassenaar's youth do not need to come into contact with coffeeshops. People who want to buy cannabis can do so in The Hague.

126674.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar wants there to be sufficient housing stock for social rental homes. The 30 percent target is also the national guideline.

126676.explanation: Lokaal Wassenaar wants to retain the neighborhood centers that currently exist. You need neighborhood centers where people can come together.

126681.explanation: You sometimes have to get larger groceries by car; some people are less mobile. Moreover, you will see people trying to park for free in residential areas; this burdens the residents there.

126682.explanation: Wassenaar currently accommodates four times as many asylum seekers as we would have to. This number might be reduced; but we want to see if Wassenaar can be compensated, for example, by fewer social rental homes for residence permit holders. This should be discussed in the council.

126626.explanation: Unless this really doesn’t work, this is an important point for us.

126627.explanation: We support public participation, but referendums often lead to disappointment because of raised expectations.

126630.explanation: The question is whether this is sufficient for a substantial solution.

126633.explanation: Plenty of space to plant trees.

126636.explanation: A laser show can achieve the New Year's Eve feeling just as well. We do that in Wassenaar-Dorp and in Kerkehout!

126637.explanation: We want an accessible rate, but free goes too far for us.

126639.explanation: The municipality must engage in conversations with entrepreneurs about this. Entrepreneurs should also be aware of this themselves, also to attract customers.

126640.explanation: We are careful with our greenery.

126641.explanation: Disagree. We introduce the Paauwpas, making this easily accessible for residents at or near the poverty line.

126644.explanation: Disagree. We introduce the Paauwpas, making this easily accessible for residents at or near the poverty line.

126648.explanation: In places where insecurity is experienced, we consider this a good plan.

126649.explanation: No objection if this is implemented, but there is no necessity for Wassenaar itself.

126650.explanation: This is not the responsibility of the municipality.

126653.explanation: We are willing to allow this as a temporary measure (personal circumstances).

126654.explanation: We want to seek this more in the (limited) height of existing buildings rather than developing new areas in green spaces.

126655.explanation: If people can contribute something back to society fitting their knowledge and experience, we find this acceptable. Work is often also beneficial for people themselves.

126657.explanation: The percentage may, in our view, be raised to 130 percent of the social minimum in applicable cases.

126658.explanation: We will continue to support youth work.

126659.explanation: There are currently sufficient (facilities), but we will monitor whether this remains so in the coming period.

126661.explanation: In our view, this is not feasible and we choose for an inclusive society in which this freedom of choice does not fit.

126664.explanation: The money from the dog tax is spent on waste bins with poop bags that must be emptied regularly. This justifies this tax.

126666.explanation: We consider limited expansion near Maaldrift negotiable. We give preference to sustainable companies during allocation.

126668.explanation: The urgency scheme is necessary in Wassenaar to comply with the legal obligation imposed by the national government. Incidentally, this currently applies to 7 percent of social housing.

126670.explanation: Provided it is proportional, we have no problem with this.

126673.explanation: We are working on a program against substance abuse (Growing up in a Promising Environment). A coffee shop does not fit with this.

126674.explanation: In Wassenaar, this is set at 30 percent.

126676.explanation: We believe there should be a balanced distribution of meeting places within Wassenaar. This does not necessarily have to be per neighborhood.

126681.explanation: We find this well explainable. The operating costs of the parking garages cost us 600,000 euros. All residents pay this now. We want those who use them to pay, including people from outside Wassenaar.

126682.explanation: We have an asylum seeker center in Wassenaar with 930 places. With that, we more than meet our responsibility.

126626.explanation: It doesn't matter. The CDA finds it important that council members are diverse, complement each other, appreciate each other, and work well together as a team.

126627.explanation: Participation can work well through good processes, where it is important that it is well guided and clear what the level of participation is: being informed, co-thinking, etc. Traffic was a nice example of a citizens' assembly.

126629.explanation: Society demands a lot from farmers, and they deserve our support. Extensive and nature-inclusive farming means less income per hectare and this can be compensated well by, for example, a (cheese-milk) farm shop on the property.

126630.explanation: The CDA supports neighborhood initiatives for sustainable energy and sees an active role for the municipality. Think of a heating initiative or a neighborhood battery, which requires both material and immaterial support.

126633.explanation: Nature and greenery are important for Wassenaar and should be preserved and, where possible, strengthened. So, at least plant one tree for every felled (dead) tree.

126636.explanation: Now that the fireworks ban has come into effect, it is good to organize a beautiful central fireworks display.

126637.explanation: If it is free, it will be used excessively. We have seen this with the disposal of bulky waste. It is good to ask for a small fee.

126640.explanation: Initiatives can always exist, but they must be evaluated on their merits. In general, the CDA pursues more affordable housing, and perhaps an initiative can serve the public interest.

126641.explanation: There are already schemes for this in Wassenaar. The CDA finds sport very important, especially for youth, and wants to double youth subsidies for sports clubs in the coming period. Sport is good, social and healthy, and you cannot start early enough.

126644.explanation: Subsidies already exist for this, and they are broader than just music lessons. And certainly music enriches upbringing, but not every child is interested in it.

126648.explanation: Cameras provide a feeling of safety and help prevent and solve crime. However, cameras must be installed carefully and there must be a clear reason.

126649.explanation: It is important to have a safe home for women who are victims of domestic violence, but we address this regionally. Nevertheless, there can be a domestic violence shelter in Wassenaar.

126650.explanation: Banks provide loans. As CDA, we commit to building additional affordable housing in Wassenaar, for example along Zonneveldweg or on the Jozefschool site. Preferably sooner rather than later, because it all takes too long.

126653.explanation: Holiday homes in Wassenaar are and remain for vacationers.

126654.explanation: Wassenaar is surrounded by dunes, ridges, and meadows. We want to keep it that way.

126655.explanation: Social assistance benefits are not given without reason and come with no obligations - this belongs to our welfare state. However, it is good to activate and motivate people receiving benefits to participate meaningfully in society; paid or voluntary work.

126657.explanation: The CDA sets this income limit at 125 percent of the subsistence level.

126658.explanation: The CDA wants to invest in youth through a youth council and additional funds for sports. Because youth are the future and also have good ideas.

126659.explanation: The CDA focuses on the energy transition and climate adaptation, which includes more public electric charging points.

126661.explanation: CDA supports free choice where possible. But primarily, it is important that good care is provided in a timely manner.

126664.explanation: Dog tax is an old tax for pets and affects only dog owners. Other pets, such as horses that use public outdoor spaces and also impact these areas, are exempt.

126666.explanation: Maaldrift may and can expand a little, but it remains limited.

126668.explanation: CDA believes that status holders have urgency for social housing, just like many other groups. However, CDA wants to focus on extra flexible housing, where there is room for young people and status holders, to relieve the regular social housing.

126670.explanation: Agricultural land is for agriculture, not for solar panels. CDA does want to encourage solar panels and significantly relax regulations regarding the installation of solar panels on roofs.

126673.explanation: Coffeeshops do not belong in Wassenaar.

126674.explanation: CDA wants to build more social housing in Wassenaar, but funds are needed for this. To that end, CDA wants to establish a compensation fund where developers who do not build according to the standard contribute financially to the fund.

126676.explanation: CDA aims for three good community centers spread throughout the village. These community centers can be used for social activities but also as support points during emergencies such as power outages and lack of drinking water.

126681.explanation: The Wassenaar town center will retain free parking.

126682.explanation: The number of asylum seekers is fixed until 2027. After that, CDA believes the number should decrease towards the amount mentioned in the distribution law.

126626.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants the municipal executive to be a good reflection of our society. This includes a balanced distribution between men and women. At the same time, we consider suitability and expertise of candidates, so that quality and cooperation remain paramount.

126627.explanation: D66 Wassenaar chooses citizen assemblies instead of referenda because we believe residents deserve more than a simple yes or no. In a citizen assembly, people have the space to jointly find solutions, make well-informed choices, and come to supported proposals.

126629.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to encourage local entrepreneurship and give farmers the space to start a shop, campsite, or other small-scale activity alongside their business. Only when there are compelling reasons, such as risks to nature or safety, can the municipality intervene.

126630.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to actively involve residents in the energy transition. This is not only good for the climate but also strengthens the local community. Therefore, we encourage initiatives, with financial support for our residents where necessary.

126633.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants nature and biodiversity in our village to be strengthened. For every tree cut down, appropriate compensation must be made to preserve greenery. Not necessarily a tree for a tree, but there must be a careful plan that restores or increases natural value.

126636.explanation: If a national fireworks ban is introduced, we believe the municipality must offer a good alternative. Preferably an innovative and sustainable solution, such as a drone show, provided this is feasible within the available budget and carefully balanced for safety, environment, and support.

126637.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to use waste smarter and encourage residents to produce less waste. We choose clear tariffs and reward good behavior. Moreover, sorting materials is better possible at the recycling center than at home.

126639.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for accessibility. The municipality has a role model function and actively promotes accessibility, but financial support from the municipality is not necessary.

126640.explanation: This can lead to unwanted effects such as speculation and high sale prices, jeopardizing affordable housing and the desired distribution (social rent, mid-range and expensive owner-occupied homes).

126641.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants school buildings to become better and more modern. We will quickly implement the plan for school accommodation so that schools are healthy, sustainable, and accessible.

126644.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants every child to participate in sports, culture, and creative activities such as music lessons. We focus on a rich school day in which schools collaborate with clubs and institutions, supported by subsidies and the Wassenaar pass.

126648.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to increase safety through cooperation and visible presence in the neighborhood, not by adding more cameras. Camera surveillance infringes on privacy and is only appropriate in clear cases of necessity, such as structural nuisance.

126649.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants victims of domestic violence to always receive quick and safe help. We choose cooperation with regional partners to ensure shelter and support are well organized so that no one falls between the cracks.

126650.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants starters to have a fair chance in the housing market, without their future depending on financial support from parents. A starter loan fits with our commitment to affordable housing and circulation through schemes such as KoopGarant.

126653.explanation: Allowing permanent residence in existing vacation homes without changing the zoning can be a pragmatic option in this time of housing shortage. Of course, we will take firm action in cases of nuisance or criminal practices, to maintain safety and livability in vacation parks.

126654.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to create enough space for housing construction, also outside the current village borders where possible. This will result in more affordable and suitable homes for starters, families, and the elderly.

126655.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants social assistance to be based on trust and tailored solutions, not on coercion. People deserve individual support that fits their situation so they can gradually participate in society again.

126657.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants help to reach residents who really need it, with tailored solutions and a strong social safety net. Raising the income limit is irresponsible as more people already live in poverty and living costs are rising.

126658.explanation: Young people are the future of our village and deserve opportunities to grow up healthily and develop themselves. Therefore, we focus on accessible sports and cultural activities, collaboration with local organizations, and initiatives such as the youth restaurant.

126659.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants sufficient charging points to encourage electric driving. We focus on places where there is currently a shortage and combine this with smart solutions such as charging stations at bicycle parking and public transport hubs.

126661.explanation: If the urgency is high, this is not always possible. The care provider must do everything possible to provide appropriate help. D66 believes we can expect professional caregivers to provide good assistance across all cultures, religions, or beliefs.

126664.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants municipal taxes to be fair and logical. The revenues are not directly used for the costs the municipality incurs for this. It is also unclear whether the collection and enforcement costs outweigh the revenues.

126666.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to use the scarce space in our village for housing and nature, not for expanding industrial sites. We choose smart and sustainable use of existing work locations and the greening of outdated real estate.

126668.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants status holders to start their lives in our village quickly. The municipality has the legal obligation to assign a number of refugees determined by the national government each year.

126670.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to fully invest in solar energy, preferably on roofs and other unused surfaces. We find using valuable agricultural land for solar parks undesirable; that space is needed for food production and nature.

126673.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to continue the program Growing Up in a Prosperous Environment to increase opportunities for healthy upbringing. Allowing a coffeeshop is not part of this.

126674.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants new construction projects to contribute to a fair housing market. This way, we ensure affordable homes for people with smaller budgets and keep Wassenaar a village where everyone can live.

126676.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants neighborhoods to have places where people meet, share ideas, and organize activities together. Neighborhood centers provide space for care, support, and their own initiatives, so young and old naturally find each other.

126681.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants to keep the center attractive and accessible. Paid parking does not fit with this.

126682.explanation: D66 Wassenaar wants our village to remain a welcoming community. We actively commit to the shelter of refugees and asylum seekers and ensure they can quickly participate in society, for example by starting education and work early.

126627.explanation: Residents should also be able to vote on important issues between elections.

126629.explanation: That must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

126630.explanation: Why should all residents have to pay for other residents' projects?

126633.explanation: Wassenaar must remain the green oasis in the Randstad.

126636.explanation: Only if a majority of the residents are in favor.

126637.explanation: This keeps Wassenaar a clean municipality.

126639.explanation: Entrepreneurs themselves are responsible for making their buildings more accessible to people with disabilities.

126640.explanation: Wassenaar must cherish its agricultural areas. Only in exceptional cases can building be permitted in specific instances.

126641.explanation: For children living below the poverty line, there are charitable funds, such as the Leergeld Foundation and the Wassenaar Fund.

126644.explanation: For children living below the poverty line, there are charitable funds, such as the Leergeld Foundation and the Wassenaar Fund.

126649.explanation: Not necessary. That is a statutory task for larger municipalities like The Hague, Delft, and Leiden.

126650.explanation: Why should all residents have to contribute to loans of other residents?

126653.explanation: Holiday homes can be used to reduce the housing shortage.

126654.explanation: There is a housing shortage, so new construction should be allowed where it does not harm Wassenaar as a green oasis in the Randstad.

126655.explanation: A counter-performance is reasonable, if possible.

126657.explanation: No, why? The current approach is fine.

126658.explanation: No, youth work does not receive that much subsidy, and Wassenaar can certainly afford to invest in its youth.

126661.explanation: As much as possible, appropriate care should be provided to care recipients.

126664.explanation: We cannot afford to lose this income. However, the municipality must ensure good facilities such as dog walking areas and their maintenance.

126666.explanation: To a limited extent and exclusively for Wassenaar-based businesses.

126668.explanation: No! We do this now and it must come to an end. Wassenaar residents currently wait far too long for social rental housing.

126670.explanation: Agricultural land must remain agricultural land. Solar panels belong on roofs.

126673.explanation: Drugs destroy more than we would like.

126674.explanation: At least 30 percent is already the target of Wassenaar. That is fine as it is.

126676.explanation: Community centers are good for social cohesion and prevent loneliness.

126681.explanation: Paid parking could be the death blow for the village center of Wassenaar and drives people to shopping centers where parking is free, such as Mariahoeve and Leidschendam.

126626.explanation: Equal representation is part of a modern, inclusive local democracy; a gender balance (and diversity) strengthens trust and decision quality.

126627.explanation: Residents must be able to directly participate in discussions and decisions. Referenda, with good information and clear rules, can increase engagement.

126629.explanation: Space for secondary activities should be possible, but only if nature, traffic pressure, and the living environment are taken into account. We encourage agricultural entrepreneurs to transition to sustainable and regenerative farming and simplify permits for future-proof business models.

126630.explanation: Energy cooperatives and neighborhood initiatives accelerate the transition. The municipality can help with financing/knowledge (including EU programs) for generation and storage.

126633.explanation: Trees are crucial for health, biodiversity, and cooling. Therefore, we compensate for cutting down trees generously and increase the greenery.

126636.explanation: With the introduction of the fireworks ban, the municipality can play a nice role in maintaining the fireworks tradition appropriately, with sufficient attention to the environment and surroundings.

126637.explanation: We want to manage waste streams circularly. By asking for a small contribution, municipal costs are covered, people are encouraged to sort better, and reuse is promoted. Users also pay; if this is not charged, general waste taxes will increase.

126639.explanation: Accessibility is a basic right. The municipality can (co)finance and advise so that shops and hospitality are accessible to everyone, in line with EU standards.

126640.explanation: Additional housing is needed, but green spaces must be protected. Only small-scale and landscape-inclusive development with clear limits against speculation. We do facilitate the construction of 'kangoeroewoningen' (in-law units), also on agricultural land.

126641.explanation: Sports remain accessible through existing arrangements like 'Fit in Wassenaar' (part of Kans Lokaal). We encourage exercise for everyone without excluding groups or creating extra administration.

126644.explanation: Cultural education connects and develops talent. With subsidies, we make music lessons accessible to every child, regardless of income.

126648.explanation: Camera surveillance should focus only on real hotspots and be subject to strict privacy rules and periodic evaluation. Prevention and social safety remain leading; large-scale surveillance is rejected.

126649.explanation: Violence against women requires a strong approach and safe shelter. A (regional) women's shelter is part of this, with good aftercare.

126650.explanation: Volt stands for young families and wants to expand options for starters by promoting subsequent moves and providing suitable new construction. We want to give priority to our elderly who want to downsize. One such move typically leads to an average of 4 to 5 moves and a larger supply for starters.

126653.explanation: Holiday homes as primary residences reduce recreational offerings and often provide lower living quality. We prefer to build real, affordable homes for starters and families, but do not exclude an intermediate step.

126654.explanation: First within the boundaries: densify where possible and appropriate, without damaging protected village views. Only expand, including nature-inclusive, when there is demonstrable need.

126655.explanation: Participation is good, but no mandatory counter-performance as standard. We focus on guidance, education and suitable work based on trust and income security.

126657.explanation: A higher income threshold prevents working people with a tight budget from just missing out. This helps prevent debts and related health effects and strengthens income security.

126658.explanation: Young people are the future. Youth work prevents problems, strengthens participation and increases opportunities; you do not cut funding here.

126659.explanation: More charging points accelerate electric driving. We combine this with smart solutions such as cable ducts and (in time) bi-directional charging to relieve the grid.

126661.explanation: Choice in healthcare is important, but ‘always’ is not feasible. Care must fit the individual, within reasonable availability and quality standards. This prevents unnecessary duplication among care providers and keeps costs lower for everyone.

126664.explanation: The dog tax covers the costs of dog waste bags and the associated municipal logistics. The user pays, which can be fair and efficient in this case.

126666.explanation: We choose smart, sustainable businesses through densification. Expansion must not come at the expense of green spaces and livability, for example by topping up houses and business premises. We want to relax restrictive rules on building heights outside the center.

126668.explanation: A home is crucial for integration. Limited priority helps people move out of shelters, accelerates participation and offers opportunities for the local economy. We work within national guidelines and with fairness in mind.

126670.explanation: Volt Wassenaar wants to gradually develop more measures to make Wassenaar a climate-neutral municipality faster. We do this by stimulating sustainable solutions rather than banning them.

126673.explanation: Banning does not work: it shifts problems elsewhere. Drug policy must be factual: regulate, spread out and enforce on nuisance and safety. There are sufficient good examples outside Wassenaar that can serve as a basis.

126674.explanation: Volt Wassenaar supports a balanced housing mix (30 percent social, 40 percent mid-range, 30 percent free sector) so that starters and middle incomes have a chance.

126676.explanation: Meeting places are important, but not every neighborhood needs a new building. We strengthen existing spots (library, Warenar, De Paauw) and make smart use of schools and town hall.

126681.explanation: Paid parking in the center creates a waterbed effect to the surrounding ring and the related search traffic. We want to make space for cyclists and pedestrians, but believe this is possible with existing underground facilities and blue zones.

126682.explanation: Wassenaar already does more than the housing distribution law requires and we are proud of that. Volt Wassenaar focuses on strengthening integration of asylum seekers and labor migrants.

statements.title: The municipality must introduce paid parking in the center.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Wassenaar must have increased camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The new college of mayor and aldermen must have an equal number of men and women.

titleParts.glossary: The college of mayor and aldermen, the municipality’s executive board.
The mayor leads the municipality and chairs the municipal council. The mayor is not elected but appointed by the national government, after recommendation by the municipal council.

titleParts.text: en wethouders moet het aantal mannen en vrouwen gelijk zijn.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: At least 30% of the newly built homes in Wassenaar must be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Wassenaar moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social rental housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: The residents of Wassenaar must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Wassenaar moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should financially help business owners to make their buildings accessible to people with disabilities.

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

titleParts.text: helpen om hun gebouw toegankelijker te maken voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Something that limits you in daily life. A disability can be physical or intellectual or relate to hearing or sight, or combinations of these.

statements.title: The municipality should cut spending on youth work.

titleParts.glossary: Spending less money.
People and organizations that help young people in the municipality with various things.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Wassenaar must provide a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
In Wassenaar the business parks may expand.
The municipality must prevent the opening of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
Every neighborhood in the village must have a community center.
Wassenaar must limit the reception of asylum seekers to the number prescribed by national law.

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

titleParts.text: beperken tot het aantal dat de landelijke wet voorschrijft.
